title: Tim Jordan
content:
Tim Jordan (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as captain of the Los Angeles Lkers when they won the BBA championship in 1987.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Tim Jordan recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1987 playoffs.

Awards and honors.

Following the BBA championship, Tim Jordan received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Legacy.

Historians of the Los Angeles Lkers cite Tim Jordan's 1987 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Harrowgate Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Professional career.

Tim Jordan joined the Los Angeles Lkers and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1982s. By 1986, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Early life and college career.

Tim Jordan played college basketball in Harrowgate before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as captain.

Playing style.

As captain, Tim Jordan preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Los Angeles Lkers switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the BBA championship, Tim Jordan remained with the Los Angeles Lkers for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.
